notes.note: Lucius Munatius Plancus, governor of Transalpine Gaul in 43 BC; the letter is from him to Cicero, the salutation following the Roman epistolary convention of naming sender before recipient.

notes.anchor: sixth day before the Kalends of May
notes.note: Ante diem sextum Kalendas Maias = 26 April 43 BC. Roman dates counted inclusively backward from the Kalends (1st), Nones, or Ides of the month.

notes.anchor: I crossed the Rhone with the army by forced marches
notes.note: Plancus was marching from Transalpine Gaul toward northern Italy to support the Senatorial forces against Antony; the Rhone crossing was a critical logistical milestone demonstrating his commitment to the Republic.
Modern Vienne (France), on the Rhone south of Lyon; the principal city of the Allobroges and an important Roman town in Transalpine Gaul, serving as Plancus's forward staging point.

notes.anchor: if Lepidus does not obstruct me
notes.note: M. Aemilius Lepidus, governor of Narbonese Gaul and Nearer Spain, whose loyalty was already suspect; Plancus feared Lepidus might physically block his army's route or defect to Antony, which Lepidus did in May 43 BC.
